text.title: Team Mode CLI Flag (`--team-config` Overlay) Failure Modes
text.summary: 3 antipatterns observed in real deployments of this methodology. Each pairs symptom + root cause + concrete fix.

antipattern.symptom: CLI loads the overlay by file path and never checks signature. Attacker plants a forged overlay → model whitelist swapped silently.
antipattern.fix: Enforce ed25519 verification before applying; reject mismatch with non-zero exit.

antipattern.symptom: `signed_by: team-platform`. Three quarters later nobody knows who to ask about the overlay.
antipattern.root-cause: Owner field set to a group, not a person.
antipattern.fix: Reject anything other than a person handle in `signed_by`; the team-platform key signs the file, but the human owns it.

antipattern.symptom: Overlay last reviewed 14 months ago; references retired model.
antipattern.root-cause: No staleness check at load time.
antipattern.fix: Emit a warning when `last_reviewed > 90 days` and a hard error after 180.
